conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. Their name is Alex, and they're 6 years old. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd like to gather a bit more detail to properly assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

First, could you tell me: are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, does Alex live with you and do you look after them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me and I look after them full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Let me ask a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you or Alex currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Now, just to clarify - is Alex also living in the UK with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex lives here in the UK with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. I have a few more questions to ensure I assess your eligibility correctly.

Is Alex currently in hospital or in any form of local authority care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex was in local authority care for a short period recently - just a couple of months - but they're back home with me now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. To understand this situation better, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently still in local authority care, or are they now living with you full-time again and no longer in care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is back living with me full-time now and is no longer in care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Let me just read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information about your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I have just one more question to complete my assessment:

Is anyone else (such as another parent or family member) currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I can now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize your situation:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you in the UK and you look after them full-time
- You are responsible for Alex
- Alex was in local authority care for a short period (a couple of months) but is now back living with you full-time and no longer in care
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Based on the official guidance, you **are eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex. 

The key points are:
- You qualify because you're responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you in the UK
- Since Alex is now back home with you full-time and is no longer in local authority care, the previous care period doesn't affect your current eligibility
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and since nobody else is claiming for Alex, you can make the claim

You should be able to apply for Child Benefit for Alex successfully.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your claim?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for looking after them full-time. Although Alex was in local authority care for a short period recently, they are now back home and no longer in care. According to the eligibility rules, a person qualifies for Child Benefit if they are responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK, and they live with the child. The claimant meets all these criteria. Additionally, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 6), who lives with them full-time in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Although Alex was in local authority care briefly, they are now back home. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
